text.title: Persona Building Process and Data Requirements
text.summary: Five-step evidence-anchored persona building process: data gathering, pattern clustering, segment definition, persona construction, and validation with a falsification clause.

rule.statement: Run at least 10 customer interviews before creating any persona; fewer interviews produce stereotypes, not archetypes.
rule.rationale: Below 10 interviews, pattern confidence is too low to distinguish signal from individual variation.

rule.statement: Apply a no-demographics-first rule: write goals and frustrations before any demographic detail.
rule.rationale: Demographics trigger stereotype completion in both humans and LLMs; goals and frustrations derived first stay grounded in interview evidence.

rule.statement: Every bullet in every persona must cite the supporting interview IDs. Never invent demographic detail not present in the transcripts.
rule.rationale: Hallucinated quotes and invented demographics are the most damaging failure mode — they justify wrong decisions with false specificity.

section.title: Clustering dimensions
section.p: Cluster utterances across four dimensions: Goals (what they want to achieve), Frustrations (what blocks them), Behaviours (how they work and buy), Information Sources (where they learn and get influenced). Each cluster needs a one-sentence pattern, supporting interview IDs, and three verbatim quotes.

rule.statement: Propose at most one primary persona and one negative persona (who NOT to target) per research round without explicit approval to add more.
rule.rationale: Multiple personas at pre-traction stage dilute focus; negative personas are consistently skipped and are as valuable as positive ones for positioning.

rule.statement: Pair the persona doc with one paragraph titled "How we would notice this persona is wrong" — a falsification clause.
rule.rationale: Without a falsification clause, personas accumulate institutional momentum and survive past their useful life.

rule.statement: Refresh cycle: minor update quarterly (interview signals, support ticket categories); major rebuild on pivots or new markets.
rule.rationale: Personas frozen at year-one data keep echoing in year-three decisions against a different reality.

rule.statement: Show the draft persona to actual customers and ask if they recognize themselves; measure recognition rate before using for decisions.
rule.rationale: Recognition testing is the fastest validation that the archetype maps to reality rather than to the research team's assumptions.

rule.statement: Distribute the persona where decisions are made: PM Notion, marketing brief template, sales playbook. A persona-only document gathers dust.
rule.rationale: Personas only change behaviour when they are embedded in the artifacts people already use for decisions.
